description: Static golden example for the cross-binding determinism contract (CONFORMANCE_SPEC.md §5.5, RFC semiring-faq-unified-ir §5.7). Each fixture pins a byte-identical serialized index set and the canonical dense-ID array a conforming producer MUST emit for the value-invention primitives (skolem / distinct / rank) and group-by aggregate joins. Adversarial input variants (permuted, duplicated, reversed orientation) MUST collapse to the identical golden output, proving order-, duplicate-, and orientation-independence. The M2 joins and M3 relational engine have landed: each binding (Julia / Rust / Python) ships a thin adapter (scripts/run-determinism-conformance.py + EARTHSCI_DETERMINISM_ADAPTER_<BINDING>) and the runner asserts every adapter's serialized index set + base-normalized dense IDs are byte-identical to this golden — for the canonical input AND every adversarial variant. `bindings_required` lists all three, so a missing or mismatching producer fails the gate. The embedded reference implementation remains the --self-test green check.

rank_base_pin._doc: rank assigns dense IDs by position in the sorted distinct sequence. Conformance asserts on the CANONICAL numbering (0-based, language-neutral position). Each binding emits in its native base and the adapter declares it; the harness normalizes reported IDs to canonical via (reported - emission_base) before comparison. Go/TS receive the additive schema only (no evaluator/producer) and are N/A here.

fixtures.doc: Enumerate the unique undirected edges of a 2-triangle mesh (two faces sharing edge {2,3}). Directed edges are derived by traversing consecutive vertices of each face (with wraparound); skolem canonicalizes each to (min,max); distinct sorts by total order and drops adjacent duplicates; rank assigns dense IDs by position. The shared edge appears in both faces with OPPOSITE orientation and MUST collapse to a single canonical key.

Distinct over a directed relation: orientation is significant, so (1,2) and (2,1) are DISTINCT keys (contrast edge_enumeration). skolem preserves tuple order; exact-duplicate arcs are dropped by distinct; output is sorted by total order.

Group-by aggregate under the integer (+) semiring with STRING keys. Exercises (a) total order on strings = Unicode code-point (UTF-8 byte) order, NOT locale collation: code-point order is 'B'(U+0042) < 'Z'(U+005A) < 'a'(U+0061), whereas a case-insensitive locale would interleave 'a' among the capitals; (b) associativity+commutativity of (+) — permuting the input rows cannot change any bucket sum; (c) emit sorted by canonical key, never hash-bucket order. dense_ids_canonical ranks the emitted (sorted) distinct keys.

negative_controls.doc: A producer that emits distinct results in first-seen / hash-iteration order instead of sorted total order MUST be flagged as a mismatch by the harness. Validated in scripts/run-determinism-conformance.py self-test by feeding the comparator a deliberately mis-ordered index set and asserting it is rejected.

Floats are forbidden in keys. A producer that admits a float key component MUST raise rather than silently bucket on a platform-dependent float repr. Validated in the self-test by asserting the reference primitives reject a float-keyed tuple.
